title: Sphaerocionium lobatoalatum
intro: Sphaerocionium lobatoalatum is a type of lichen, a composite organism consisting of a fungus and a photosynthetic partner, typically an algae or cyanobacterium. The health benefits of Sphaerocionium lobatoalatum include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including wounds and infections. Bioactive constituents of this plant include lobatoalatin, a unique compound that has been isolated from its extracts, as well as other secondary metabolites, such as depsides and depsidones, which are known for their biological activities. Sphaerocionium lobatoalatum is typically used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.

It is classified under the phylum Marchantiophyta, which also includes other liverworts. Within the class Marchantiophyta, Sphaerocionium lobatoalatum is further classified under the order Marchantes and the family Sphaerocionitaceae.
